summary: Iteration 5 — Validated all newly added pre-publication features on the live preview (https://order-basket-5.preview.emergentagent.com). Backend pytest 10/11 PASS (90.9%) in /app/backend/tests/test_new_features_v5.py. Confirmed: (a) POST /api/admin/products persists stock_unit, original_price, and auto-sets initial_stock_grams=total_stock_grams; (b) PATCH /api/admin/products/{id} resets initial_stock_grams on restock; (c) GET /api/shop/hours now exposes delivery_disabled_today at the top level AND inside status; toggling PUT /api/admin/shop/hours with today's day flipping delivery_disabled=true correctly flips the flag back via the next GET; (d) POST /api/orders with delivery_mode='delivery' is rejected with HTTP 400 (message contains 'livraison') when delivery_disabled_today=true; same payload with delivery_mode='pickup' returns 200; (e) variant_grams() now handles '1 L', '0.5 L', '5 unités' AND legacy '5 g' — shared total_stock_grams decremented correctly in all three cases; (f) PUT /api/admin/categories/reorder and /api/admin/products/reorder persist sort_order — GET returns the new order, restored at teardown. Frontend smoke: /catalog has catalog-view-toggle that flips Grid↔List, value persists in localStorage key 'catalog_view_mode_v1' (verified value='list'). Home shows '🔥 En promotion' (matching products exist); 'À venir' and 'En fin de stock' sections correctly absent because no products currently match (coming_soon=false everywhere, no products under 20% of initial). Checkout shows Livraison + Sur place buttons; the orange 'Livraison indisponible' banner is correctly hidden because delivery_disabled_today is false on the restored server state — banner toggle visually verified earlier in the toggle round-trip API test. ONE BACKEND BUG FOUND: POST /api/admin/products with no stock_unit returns HTTP 500 instead of defaulting to 'g' — see RCA. Carried-over RN warnings still present (shadow*, pointerEvents, stale 'admin' route name in (tabs)/_layout). No mocked APIs.

critical.issue: When stock_unit is omitted from the payload, server returns HTTP 500 (pydantic ValidationError: 'Input should be a valid string [...], input_value=None'). The Product model declares stock_unit: str = 'g' (non-Optional) but ProductIn declares stock_unit: Optional[str] = None — ProductIn passes None into Product, which fails validation. Result: any admin creating a product via the form without explicitly selecting a stock unit gets a 500. Fix: either change ProductIn.stock_unit default to 'g' OR strip None values before constructing Product in admin_create_product (server.py:1581-1588).

react_native_issues.issue: Console warning: '"shadow*" style props are deprecated. Use "boxShadow".' Reference: /app/frontend/src/theme.ts.
Console warning: 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated. Use style.pointerEvents'.
Console warning: '[Layout children]: No route named "admin" exists in nested children …'. Reference: /app/frontend/app/(tabs)/_layout.tsx.

react_native_issues.component: Expo SDK patch versions still behind (carried over)
react_native_issues.issue: expo@54.0.34 (~54.0.35), expo-font@14.0.11 (~14.0.12), expo-router@6.0.23 (~6.0.24). Run `npx expo install expo expo-font expo-router`.

action_items: [HIGH — backend bug] Fix POST /api/admin/products 500 when stock_unit is omitted. In /app/backend/server.py:1545-1560 change `ProductIn.stock_unit: Optional[str] = None` → `stock_unit: str = 'g'`. OR, in admin_create_product (line ~1582), filter Nones before constructing the Product: `data = {k: v for k, v in payload.dict().items() if v is not None or k in ('original_price','total_stock_grams','low_stock_threshold_grams','initial_stock_grams','unit')}`.

[Frontend nice-to-have] Verify on a real test scenario that creating a product with coming_soon=true makes the home 'À venir' section appear, and that a product whose stock is decremented below 20% of initial_stock_grams makes the 'En fin de stock' section appear. The conditional rendering logic itself appears to be correct (both sections are hidden when zero matches), but live regression of the trigger conditions was not exercised in this iteration because no products in the DB satisfy them.

[Carried over from iter 3-4] Migrate shadow* tokens in /app/frontend/src/theme.ts to boxShadow; remove stale Stack.Screen name="admin" in (tabs)/_layout.tsx; replace deprecated pointerEvents prop with style.pointerEvents; run `npx expo install expo expo-font expo-router` to align Expo SDK patch versions.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py:1545-1559 — ProductIn and Product disagree on stock_unit nullability. ProductIn.stock_unit: Optional[str] = None vs Product.stock_unit: str = 'g'. Pydantic v2 strict mode rejects None for str. This is the root cause of the 500 above.

/app/backend/server.py:1581-1588 — admin_create_product passes payload.dict() directly into Product(**data). If you want POST to accept partial bodies, you must drop None for the Optional ProductIn fields whose Product counterpart is non-Optional. Cleanest fix: align both models on `str = 'g'`.

/app/backend/server.py:1592-1608 — admin_update_product uses exclude_unset=True, which is correct (avoids overwriting stored values with None). However it still sets initial_stock_grams=None when total_stock_grams is explicitly cleared (line 1601). Spec says reset on restock, so that branch is fine. Note: there's no guard against negative total_stock_grams — admin could set -5 today; consider Pydantic Field(ge=0).

/app/backend/server.py:167-196 — _GRAM_LABEL_RE + _LEADING_NUM_RE fallback regex confirmed working on '1 L', '0.5 L', '5 unités', and legacy '5 g'. Field is still named `grams` even when the stock_unit is 'L' or 'unité' — that's a documented compromise (comment on line 154-156). Long-term consider renaming Product.total_stock_grams → total_stock_amount.

/app/backend/server.py:2850-2867 — GET /api/shop/hours correctly emits delivery_disabled_today at BOTH top level AND inside status. PUT /api/admin/shop/hours preserves the per-day delivery_disabled bool (line 2895).
/app/backend/server.py:551-566 — Order creation now correctly short-circuits with HTTP 400 if delivery_disabled today. The fallback `except Exception: pass` keeps DB-error resilience, good.

/app/backend/server.py:1676-1689 — Reorder endpoints persist sort_order by index. Note: PUT loops with await per id — N round-trips. For the 8-30 categories/products typical here it's fine, but a bulk_write with UpdateOne ops would be O(1) network calls.

/app/frontend/app/(tabs)/catalog.tsx — view-mode persistence verified at runtime: AsyncStorage key 'catalog_view_mode_v1' = 'list' after toggle; React-Native-Web maps AsyncStorage to localStorage so the value survives reload.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_new_features_v5.py (new — 11 pytest cases covering all P0 spec items)

success_rate.backend: 90.9% (10/11 PASS — the single FAIL is a real bug, not a flaky test)
success_rate.frontend: Smoke OK — all targeted testIDs and storage keys verified; deeper UI flows of admin reorder arrows / shop-hours checkbox not exercised this iteration but backend-side persistence is proven

seed_data_creation: Each test creates and deletes its own product within the same test class (TEST_StockUnitProduct, TEST_Patch, TEST_Default, TEST_LitreProduct, TEST_UnitesProduct, TEST_LegacyGram). Net-zero seed data — every fixture cleans up. Note: 'TEST_Default' creation 500s so no cleanup needed for that case. One leftover side effect: a few TEST_v5 guest orders persist in db.orders since /api/orders has no DELETE for guest orders.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend test file is /app/backend/tests/test_new_features_v5.py — re-run after the stock_unit fix to see 11/11. Frontend smoke covered: /catalog view-toggle + localStorage key 'catalog_view_mode_v1', /checkout banner conditional (banner correctly hidden when delivery flag off). NOT covered with deep clicks this iteration: (a) admin/products category chips + up/down reorder arrows clicked end-to-end (only backend reorder endpoint tested), (b) admin/categories arrows clicked end-to-end (only backend tested), (c) admin/shop-hours 'Livraison indisponible' checkbox click → save round-trip via the UI (only backend tested), (d) home 'À venir' / 'En fin de stock' rendering with matching products. Admin PIN 2937 still works on first try. delivery_disabled is restored to its original value (false) at end of each test that flipped it.
rca_of_issue:
RCA — POST /api/admin/products HTTP 500 when stock_unit omitted:
• Reproduction: `curl -X POST .../api/admin/products -H 'Authorization: Bearer <admin>' -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"name":"x","description":"x","price":1,"image":"x","category_id":"fleurs","category_kind":"cbd"}'` → 500 Internal Server Error.
• Backend log: `pydantic_core._pydantic_core.ValidationError: 1 validation error for Product / stock_unit / Input should be a valid string [type=string_type, input_value=None, input_type=NoneType]`.
• Root cause: model mismatch — ProductIn (server.py:1545) declares stock_unit: Optional[str] = None; Product (server.py:159) declares stock_unit: str = 'g'. payload.dict() emits stock_unit=None which then violates the Product schema in admin_create_product (line 1587 `product = Product(**data)`).
• Mitigation (one-line fix): change ProductIn.stock_unit default to `str = 'g'` (matches Product). Alternative: in admin_create_product, do `data = {k: v for k, v in payload.dict().items() if not (k == 'stock_unit' and v is None)}` before Product(**data). Either fix unblocks the 'create product without picking a unit' admin flow.
• Impact: Any admin who submits the New Product form without explicitly selecting a unit triggers a 500. This is silent on the API and produces a generic error toast on the mobile UI.
